content.problemStatement:
Define a structure named Circle to represent a circle with a radius. Write a C program to calculate the area and perimeter of a circle and display the results.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single number rrr - The radius of the circle.


Output Format

Output on a single line, two space separated numbers - The area and perimeter of the circle with given radius.
content.inputFormat: The first and only line contains a single number r (1 ≤ r ≤ 1000) – the radius of the circle.
content.outputFormat: Output two numbers separated by a space: the area and the perimeter of the circle, both rounded to exactly two decimal places.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Radius 3 → area = π·3² = 28.27, perimeter = 2·π·3 = 18.85 (both rounded to two decimals).

solution.approach: For the given radius r we use the formulas area = π·r² and perimeter = 2·π·r. The constant π is obtained as acos(-1.0) which gives a precise double representation. We compute both values in double precision and print them with exactly two digits after the decimal point. The algorithm runs in O(1) time and uses O(1) extra space.
